Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria) indicate possible renal or urinary tract pathology, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low HbA1c with normal glucose may reflect anemia or altered red cell turnover. Hyperchloremia is present. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for possible chronic kidney disease and underlying causes.
